items.title: Piyush Goyal holds key meetings with EU, Belgian leaders to deepen trade, clean energy, agriculture cooperation

Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in agriculture, clean energy, and sustainable industrial development. The meetings aimed to deepen India's strategic economic partnership with the European Union. Goyal also explored opportunities for India's gems and jewellery sector. The visit included participation in the India-EU Trade and Technology Council Ministerial Meeting.

items.title: US Senators unveil sweeping Russia sanctions bill; name India among nations facing possible tariffs

India is named among five nations potentially facing these new trade penalties. The bill aims to discourage continued energy purchases from Russia. Its passage requires approval from both the Senate and the House of Representatives.

items.title: Why Semaglutide is an inflection point for India’s generics sector?

items.summary: The cumulative shortfall in rainfall eased to 19.3% during 1 June-13 July from 38% at the end of June. However, the improvement was uneven, with east and northeast India still reporting a 35.5% deficit, followed by the south peninsula (21.5%), northwest (11.9%) and central India (8.5%).

items.summary: Several US-based NRIs who had invested in FCNR (B) deposits in 2013 allegedly failed to disclose the interest income to US tax authorities, leading to penalties and compliance headaches later. Given these concerns, Indian banks are not likely to see substantial dollar inflows from the US.

items.title: Sprng, a new thread in Grasim's growth story as Birla's flagship
items.summary: The Sprng Energy deal highlights Grasim's transformation from a manufacturing company into the Aditya Birla Group's flagship incubation platform. Like the flagships of the Adani group and Reliance Industries, it is now becoming the engine for the group's next phase of growth.

items.title: India may open thorium sector to private firms to boost nuclear energy ambitions
items.summary: India is exploring the option of privatizing its thorium sector to enhance critical mineral supply chains and advance its nuclear energy initiatives. Legislative measures are being considered to enable selective coastal monazite sand mining for thorium extraction. Additionally, there will be a focus on accelerating research to effectively transform thorium into uranium-233, aiming for significant domestic production increases.

items.title: Fairfax to acquire IDBI Bank stake in $5.5 billion deal, biggest foreign investment in Indian bank
items.summary: Fairfax Holdings aims to secure a major stake in IDBI Bank through an improved offer. The collaboration with the government and LIC is set to yield about fifty-three thousand crore rupees from their combined stakes. This groundbreaking investment will set a new record as the largest foreign influx into an Indian bank.

items.title: Drugmakers ready response as regulator targets brand name extensions
items.summary: The DCGI has warned pharma associations of strict action against the practice of extending a primary brand name to treat a completely different illness.

items.title: Wholesale inflation hits record 9.9% in June on fuel, food prices
items.summary: India's wholesale inflation surged to a record 9.9 percent in June. Fuel and power prices rose significantly, contributing most to this increase. Food inflation also climbed to an eighteen-month high, impacting the overall index. This rise occurred while retail inflation also showed an upward trend.

items.title: Input tax credit relief for companies on staff cars, insurance likely
items.summary: Companies may soon claim input tax credit on employee vehicles. Group health and life insurance policies for staff are also included. The GST Council's law committee proposed these changes last week. These proposals aim to ease business operations and reduce costs. The GST Council will consider these reforms in an upcoming meeting.

items.title: NEW ISP: 14 of 19 sectors see double-digit growth in April
items.summary: India's formal services sector began the new fiscal year strongly in April. Fourteen of nineteen sectors tracked by the new Index of Service Production showed growth. Accommodation and food services led this expansion, growing over thirty-seven percent year-on-year. Retail trade and administrative support services also posted significant double-digit increases.

items.title: Rice, oilseed, pulses prices rise as Kharif crop sowing declines
items.summary: Retail prices are rising due to reduced sowing of key kharif crops. Inflation has increased for rice, oilseeds, and major pulses in June. Acreage for these crops declined significantly compared to last year. Traders are holding produce, limiting supply and pushing prices higher. Below normal rainfall further exacerbates concerns about future availability.

items.title: India bans import of goods made with forced labour
items.summary: India has amended its foreign trade policy to ban goods produced using forced labour. This move comes amid a US investigation into forced labour practices globally. The new policy prohibits imports of goods manufactured wholly or in part through forced labour. This prohibition will take effect after a 30-day period from its official gazette publication. The government can issue notifications to ban specific goods based on evidence of forced labour.

items.title: ET Graphics | Compact cars gain momentum
items.summary: Mini car sales saw a significant increase last quarter, doubling after a three-year slump. This surge followed recent Goods and Services Tax rate reductions, making these vehicles more affordable. Entry hatchbacks, under 3.6 meters, experienced a 107% rise in sales year-on-year. Previously, sales had dropped by 62% from their peak due to rising costs. The market now shows renewed demand for these economical vehicles.

items.title: Air India crash probe in final stage, report soon: Ram Mohan Naidu
items.summary: While the government is trying to ensure that the final report is released quickly, establishing the truth behind the crash remained its top priority, the minister said.

items.title: Government may let statutory bodies to regulate members in multidisciplinary partnerships
items.summary: The government is considering allowing professional bodies to regulate their members within multidisciplinary partnerships. This approach would enable existing watchdogs to oversee chartered accountants and company secretaries. Such a framework aims to facilitate the creation of larger Indian professional services firms. These firms could then better compete with global giants in the audit market.

items.title: India and UK in talks on Britain's carbon tax rules: Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal
items.summary: India and the UK are discussing Britain's carbon tax regulation. The new free trade agreement ensures market access for India's steel sector. Around eighty percent of India's steel exports will enjoy zero-duty access. This comprehensive trade pact aims for one hundred billion dollars in trade.

items.title: Navi Mumbai airport to begin international services with AI Express flight to Abu Dhabi on Wednesday
items.summary: Air India Express Abu Dhabi service will operate thrice weekly, connecting travelers directly to the UAE. The airport's international services began over six months after domestic operations commenced. This expansion strengthens connectivity across the Mumbai Metropolitan Region.

items.title: June vegetable oil imports dip 29 per cent
items.summary: The decline was primarily driven by a sharp fall in palm oil imports, which dropped 10.5% from the previous month as the price gap between palm oil and other edible oils narrowed significantly. Soyabean oil imports also fell 23%. A sharp 18-20% year-on-year increase in international prices of major cooking oils, driven by their diversion for biofuel production and an 11% depreciation of the Indian rupee also weighed on imports.

items.title: Broadcasters wary as TV services face telecom-style rules
items.summary: New draft rules propose telecom-style regulation for broadcasting services and internet television, leaving broadcasters worried about internet inclusion in terrestrial transmission and IPTV definitions. Stricter penalties and authorisation requirements have also raised significant industry concerns. consulting on regulating application-based linear television distribution services.

items.title: Ikea’s India makeover: ₹11,000 crore push to expand stores and local sourcing
items.summary: By the year 2030, Ikea aims to invest close to eleven thousand crore rupees in India, launching twenty-five stores of varying sizes across the nation. The Ingka Group's commitment includes over seven thousand five hundred crore rupees for new facilities in Gurugram and Noida, set to open in 2027 and 2028. Additionally, Ikea is looking into collaboration opportunities for its existing land assets.

items.title: Textile and apparel exports take a hit in Q1
items.summary: The cumulative export of textiles and apparel during April to June 2026 registered a de-growth of -2.95% over April-June 2025. "During June 2026, textile exports increased by 9.64% over the previous year, while apparel exports registered a significant degrowth of -11.25%," said CITI in a statement.

items.title: FSSAI crackdown serves food for thought as packaged food makers seek clearer rules
items.summary: In a bid to strengthen consumer protection, India's food regulatory body is intensifying efforts against packaged food companies. The lack of clarity around definitions and guidelines for emerging ingredients poses a pressing concern. Executives from the sector are urging the need for revised definitions and clearer regulations. To meet these demands, the authority has restructured its scientific panels to create standards rooted in science, facilitating smoother operations for manufacturers.

items.title: India emerging as multi-game esports market: JioBLAST CEO Charlie Cowdrey
items.summary: India's esports market is expanding into a multi-game sector with growing publisher investments. Regulatory clarity is encouraging more game launches and ecosystem development across India. The gaming industry is projected to reach $2.4 billion by 2029 with 600 million gamers. JioBLAST is diversifying its offerings to cater to this evolving competitive landscape.

items.title: India can find alternative sources for crude, but LPG at risk: Executives
items.summary: Industry executives said India should be able to source alternative crude and LNG supplies, albeit at higher prices. LPG supplies, however, which depend heavily on imports from the Gulf, could again come under strain. One executive also warned that the renewed conflict has introduced deeper uncertainties at a time when global inventories have already been depleted after months of stock drawdowns.

items.title: Hollywood returns to India box office spotlight with Spider-Man, Nolan and more big releases
items.summary: Hollywood tentpole films are expected to boost Indian box office collections significantly. A cluster of major releases arriving within a fortnight promises robust audience engagement. Diverse themes and popular franchises like Spider-Man and The Odyssey will drive strong viewer interest. These films will be showcased extensively across premium large-format screens for maximum impact. Industry analysts estimate these four films could collectively contribute close to four hundred crore rupees.

items.title: India-UK FTA comes into effect today, unlocking duty-free access for Indian exports
items.summary: India and the UK have enacted a free trade agreement, opening significant export opportunities. AI adoption is currently in its early stages, with growth being the primary driver.
